prerequisites.notes: Re-derived correct positions from .gift [3,2,0,0,2,1] match sidecar correct_position_distribution and per-question correct_position fields exactly.

gift_strict.notes: Clean importable GIFT: $CATEGORY header, ::id:: stems, exactly one '=' per MCQ, '#' per-answer feedback, '####' general feedback inside braces. No unescaped special characters (:, =, ~, #, {, }) in stems or answer text. 6 MCQ, 0 short-answer — matches standard profile (5-6 MCQ, include_short_answer=false).

length_bias.notes: No question has correct answer > 1.20x mean distractor length (feedback stripped). Closest: Q6 correct 104 chars vs 1.2x distractor mean 116.

distractor_plausibility.notes: All 18 distractors score 3-4: each encodes a corpus-grounded beginner misconception (certainty=pattern, written-vs-found confusion, loop-step confusion, determinism of found rules, stale-rule vs overgeneralisation, length=correctness).

beginner_fairness_scope.notes: No ML/training/neural/model mechanism language anywhere (week-3 boundary respected — quiz stays in the corpus's 'rules found in repeated data' framing). Prior-vocab usage limited to week-1 (deterministic/probabilistic, algorithm) and 2.1-2.3 (specification, testable, failure conditions). 'Generalisation' and 'overgeneralisation' defined in this corpus. All stems plain-language, answerable from this topic alone.

gold_reference: .claude/references/gold-examples/quiz/2-1-what-makes-a-good-specification.quiz.gift — structure, feedback density, and misconception-grade distractors match the gold bar.
verdict: Pass — all bias / plausibility / coverage / scope checks clean.
